title: Comparison of Ultrasound- vs Landmark-Guided Injections for Musculoskeletal Pain: An Umbrella Review
research_question: What is the overall effectiveness of ultrasound-guided injections compared to landmark-guided injections for treating musculoskeletal pain?
methods_summary: Umbrella review of systematic reviews and meta-analyses
Search of PubMed, EMBASE, MEDLINE, and Web of Science from inception to March 2024
Data extraction and synthesis following PRISMA guidelines
dataset: 17 articles (4 systematic reviews and 13 meta-analyses) published between 2011 and 2023
key_findings: Ultrasound-guided injections generally more accurate than landmark-guided injections, especially in shoulder joint, bicipital groove, wrist, hip, and knee
Pain relief and functional outcomes varied across sites; ultrasound guidance notably effective for bicipital groove, wrist, hip, and knee injections
Both techniques showed low incidence of adverse effects
High heterogeneity and variable quality among included reviews (3 high, 1 moderate, 7 low, 6 critically low quality)
extracted_insights: Ultrasound guidance improves injection accuracy across multiple musculoskeletal sites, but its clinical benefit for pain and function is inconsistent and site-dependent, with strongest evidence for bicipital groove, wrist, hip, and knee injections.
